points: Resources are materials that are technologically accessible, economically feasible, and culturally acceptable, transformed by human activity into utilities.
Sustainable development, endorsed by the Rio 1992 Earth Summit and Agenda 21, focuses on development without environmental damage or compromising future needs.
Resource planning involves a complex three-step process: identification/inventory, evolving planning structures, and matching with national development goals.

summary.category: Land Resources and Utilization in India
points: India's land is relief-diverse: 43% plains (agriculture), 30% mountains (tourism/rivers), and 27% plateaus (minerals).
Net Sown Area (NSA) covers about 54% of reporting area, while forest cover (23.4%) significantly lags behind the 33% target set in 1952.
Land degradation is caused by distinct regional factors: mining (Jharkhand), overgrazing (Gujarat), and over-irrigation (Punjab/Haryana).
India features six major soil types: Alluvial (most fertile), Black (cotton-ideal), Red/Yellow, Laterite (leached), Arid (saline), and Forest soils.
Soil erosion occurs as gully or sheet erosion and is mitigated through methods like contour ploughing, terrace farming, and strip cropping.
Conservation measures like shelter belts have proven effective in stabilizing sand dunes and protecting arid regions in western India.
